Mercado Libre, Latin America's e-commerce leader, has officially discontinued its Mercado Coin loyalty token. The company announced via its Mercado Pago wallet that the token will cease operations on April 17. Users can sell it in-app, use it as purchase credit, or let it be auto-converted to local fiat. The ERC-20 token, launched in Brazil in 2022, failed to gain significant traction as a rewards mechanism.

This move follows a broader trend of failed reward tokens in the region. Notably, Brazilian neobank Nubank shut down its similar Nucoin project in late 2024 after a 97% value collapse.

Mercado Libre is not exiting crypto, however. It has pivoted to a stablecoin strategy, having launched MeliDolar (MUSD) in August 2024. This dollar-pegged stablecoin, backed by U.S. Treasury securities, now serves as the backbone of its Meli Plus loyalty program, offering users a stable store of value and cashback. The company continues to hold over $38 million in Bitcoin and maintains crypto trading services.

Mercado Coin Shutdown Analysis

- Launched Aug 2022 as ERC-20 loyalty token via Mercado Pago; rewarded purchases, usable for credits or cash-out.
- Phase-out starts April 17, 2026; users can sell, spend, or auto-convert to fiat. No official reason given.
- Reflects trend of tech firms exiting branded crypto experiments while retaining selective exposure.
- Alpha: With $38M+ BTC and active stablecoin on balance sheet, the move signals pruning low-ROI loyalty tokens to double down on proven utility—hinting corporate crypto is maturing toward infrastructure over marketing gimmicks.

Generation Investment Management has over $30B AUM and a ~$1B stake in $MELI. Here is their latest published thesis on $MELI. It is the digital backbone of Latin America.

Founded in 1999 by Marcos Galperin, it is now the most valuable listed company in the region. The leadership transition is extremely safe and smart, with Galperin moving to Executive Chairman and veteran Ariel Szarfsztejn taking over as CEO.

Meli operates in 18 countries and dominates in Brazil, Argentina, and Mexico. It is an absolute powerhouse in logistics, shipping a massive 1.8 billion items in 2024 alone. But Meli is much more than just an online store. It uses e-commerce to pull people into its fintech app, Mercado Pago, which creates massive real-world impact.

Meli is the main income source for nearly two million families, and it gave over half of its users their very first digital payment or credit offer.

Management takes very smart, data-driven risks. They offer short-term credit to unbanked users based on shopping data instead of wild guesses, leading to the highest customer satisfaction scores in the entire banking industry.

They also care deeply about the future. All their shipping packaging is recyclable or compostable. Even though they left the SBTi climate group due to the exact same complex tracking rules that affected Amazon, Meli continues to aggressively roll out green, low-emission delivery vehicles. It is a rare company that mixes extreme ambition with smart risk management and true purpose.

Pretty simple thesis. I think a big part of why MELI is so successful is because their mission statement is financial inclusion and building an ecosystem for general quality of life improvements.
